expectations.text: Recommends composition over inheritance with clear rationale
expectations.evidence: Response opens with 'Recommendation: Composition. Use composition. Avoid inheritance here.' and explains the 'is-a' relationship problem.

expectations.text: Explains why inheritance is problematic here (tight coupling, AdminService would inherit things it doesn't need)
expectations.evidence: States: 'AdminService isn't a specialized UserService, it's a different service that happens to share some cross-cutting concerns. Modelling that with inheritance misrepresents the domain.'

expectations.text: Shows how to inject shared behaviors (logger, auditor, permission checker) as dependencies
expectations.evidence: Shows UserService and AdminService both taking Logger, AuditRecorder, PermissionChecker as constructor parameters.

expectations.text: Provides a concrete code example of the composition approach
expectations.evidence: Code blocks showing both services with injected collaborators. Also shows ServiceDeps dataclass as an alternative.

expectations.text: Addresses the bulk operations concern (AdminService-specific methods don't pollute UserService)
expectations.evidence: States: 'AdminService.bulk_delete and any other admin-only methods live naturally on AdminService without polluting a shared base class or forcing UserService to know about them.'

claims.claim: Old skill produced equally complete output on this eval
claims.evidence: Both responses correctly recommend composition, explain the inheritance problems, show injection patterns, and address bulk operations. Old skill response includes the ServiceDeps dataclass bonus which is a nice touch.

suggestions.reason: Both versions pass all assertions. The key qualitative difference is that the new skill response includes an explicit Before/After code block showing the inheritance anti-pattern, while the old skill jumps straight to the solution. Consider adding assertion: 'includes an explicit code example showing the inheritance anti-pattern to avoid'.

eval_feedback.overall: Both versions pass all assertions. Qualitative difference: new skill response includes explicit before/after showing the inheritance anti-pattern, which is more instructive.
